learning_outcomes.description: collaborate in the design of lesson and practice plans for students
apply contemporary teaching and learning frameworks to instrumental teaching of your instrument in a practical setting
demonstrate reflexivity in your instrumental teaching practice

assessments.assessment_title: Lesson Sequence Planning Rationale submission and Viva Voce
assessments.due_date:
24/08/2026 - 4/09/2026

PART A DUE 24/8 18:00.

PART B VIVA VOCE TO BE SCHEDULED DURING WEEKS 5 AND 6.
assessments.task_description:
THIS ASSESSMENT IS IN TWO PARTS: PART A MUST BE COMPLETED IN ORDER TO PROGRESS TO PART B


Part A: Lesson Sequence Planning Rationale (P/F)

In Part A of Assessment 1, you will write a 600-word Lesson Sequence Planning Rationale addressing musical, pedagogical and motivational approaches to one-to-one instrumental teaching. 


Task scope:

·       The planning rationale relates to the lesson sequence of 4 x 30 minute lessons which you will video record for Assessment 2 of the course.

Your submission should include the following five sections: 

Section 1. Lesson planning (150 words)

1.1    List proposed learning material for the lesson sequence including level of performance difficulty

1.2    Clearly outline the assumed musical knowledge and instrumental skill required to commence practice of this material

1.3    List areas for musical or instrumental growth in the proposed lesson materials

Section 2: Knowledge and Perception (150 words)

1.1   Describe frameworks through which musical perception will be enhanced during the lesson sequence

1.2   Describe how musical knowledge will be expanded through the lesson sequence

Section 3: Practice strategies and Meta-cognition (150 words)

3.1   Outline practice strategies for the lesson material and explain how they connect knowledge, perception and evaluative judgement 

3.2   Explain how focus of attention is directed in the practice strategies

3.3   Describe how meta-cognitive growth will be supported through the lesson sequence

Section 4: Motivation, Pedagogies and Impact Evaluation (150 words)

4.1 Describe pedagogical approaches to be employed in lessons and how they support  motivation. 

4.2 How will lesson impact be evaluated?

The following further 3 sections are required but do not contribute to word count

1.         References 

Engage with relevant scholarship throughout, including research articles and sector or professions-based reports where relevant, and provide a list of references. Ensure all sources are real and relevant to claim and context of our assessment. References are to be:

  • Listed: Apply a consistent referencing convention (e.g., APA, MLA) and include DOI.
  • Posted: You will upload and shared a referenced items through Blackboard Discussion post (References for Assessment). If the reference is too large (a book), then post a direct link to access the text. If the referenced item is not accessible anymore, then find a new one. 


2.         Reflection on task (max 100 words)

3.         Declaration of Use of AI: How did you use AI and what tools did you use? 


Submission DUE MONDAY AUG 24, 18:00

A Submission Template is available on the course site along with a Submission link to upload your:

·       Completed Submission Template

·       All references cited in your assessment.

Upload your submission as a word document.



Part B: 10 minute Viva Voce with course teaching staff (30%)

assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessments.assessment_title: Teaching Videos, Oral Presentation (video) and Peer Feedback
assessments.due_date:
Link to teaching video 1 and lesson outline 24/08/2026 6:00 pm

Link to teaching video 2 and lesson outline 7/09/2026 6:00 pm

Link to teaching video 3 and lesson outline 21/07/2026 6:00 pm

Link to teaching video 4 and lesson outline 6/10/2026 6:00 pm

Video: Oral report 12/10/2026 6:00 pm

Peer feedback 19/10/2026 6:00 pm

All components of this assessment must be completed to pass.
assessments.mode: Activity/ Performance, Oral, Product/ Artefact/ Multimedia, Written
assessments.task_description:
This assessment is in three parts. All parts must be completed to pass the assessment.

 

PART 1 (P/F): Video submission of four 30min teaching episodes and 100 word outline of how lesson planning from assessment 1 was actioned.

You will post a link to four (4) x 30 minute video recordings of lessons with a current student to the Blackboard site Discussion board. The post should be accompanied by a 100-word outline of how the lesson planning from assessment 1 was enacted.

If it is not possible to teach a current student for this task, you may enlist a friend or family member to be your teaching subject. 

Permission must be sought prior to recording lessons and submitted to the course coordinator with an official Consent Form.

If the student is under 18, this must be filled in by a legal guardian. 


IT IS NOT POSSIBLE TO PASS PART 1 WITHOUT SUBMITTING A SIGNED CONSENT FORM.

PART 2 - Due MONDAY 12th October 18:00 (40%)

Video presentation: Oral Report on teaching practice videos (10 minute maximum: the video will be turned off after 10 minutes)

You will upload a Video Report of your experiences in the Teaching Practice Assessment to the Course Blackboard Site 'Discussion Board'. 

The video report should address the outcomes in lessons of the plans in sections 1-4 of assessment 1.

The time limit is strict: marks will be deducted for going over or under the time limit. The use of speed alteration in the video is not permitted, a normal talking speed is required.

You are advised to script and rehearse your submission before making the video. Videos must be filmed in English, with a clear view of the speaker. 

PART 3 - Due Monday 19th October, 18:00 Peer Feedback (P/F)

You will post a written 250 word feedback to the three (3) class members who submitted immediately before you on the Course Blackboard Site 'Discussion Board'. if you are the first, second or third to post, please contact the Course coordinator for instruction on which videos you should give your feedback.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Links to videos must be submitted via BlackBoard: Discussion Board: Teaching Video Links

assessments.task_description:
Viva Voce 40%

You will have an in-person conversation with course staff to discuss your learning in the course and your approaches to instrumental teaching. To pass this assessment, you will need to demonstrate an understanding of the themes of the course presented throughout the semester and how these interact with your own experiences in teaching and learning instrumental music.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
